name: create_key_namespace_diverges
code:
proof fn phi_1_create_key_namespace_diverges(req: CreateRequest)
    requires
        req.obj.metadata.namespace is Some,
        req.namespace != req.obj.metadata.namespace->0,
    ensures
        req.key().namespace != req.obj.metadata.namespace->0,
{
}
reason: CreateRequest::key uses self.namespace not self.obj.metadata.namespace, so the key can silently disagree with the object's own namespace

name: controller_owner_ref_ignores_namespace
code:
proof fn phi_4_controller_owner_ref_ignores_namespace(v1: VReplicaSetView, v2: VReplicaSetView)
    requires
        v1.metadata.name is Some,
        v2.metadata.name is Some,
        v1.metadata.uid is Some,
        v2.metadata.uid is Some,
        v1.metadata.name->0 == v2.metadata.name->0,
        v1.metadata.uid->0 == v2.metadata.uid->0,
        v1.metadata.namespace != v2.metadata.namespace,
    ensures
        v1.controller_owner_ref() == v2.controller_owner_ref(),
{
}
reason: controller_owner_ref uses only name and uid but not namespace — two VReplicaSets in different namespaces with the same name and uid produce identical owner references, conflating cross-namespace ownership

name: unmarshal_skips_state_validation
code:
proof fn phi_5_unmarshal_skips_state_validation(obj: DynamicObjectView)
    requires
        obj.kind == PodView::kind(),
        PodView::unmarshal_spec(obj.spec) is Ok,
        PodView::unmarshal_status(obj.status) is Ok,
    ensures
        PodView::unmarshal(obj) is Ok,
{
}
reason: unmarshal succeeds whenever kind/spec/status parse correctly but never checks state_validation — objects that violate domain invariants (e.g. pods with spec == None) can be successfully unmarshalled
